jobs.title: Customer Delivery Driver/Picker
jobs.description: This position is working 16 hours per week, any 2 in 6 days including bank holidays. Our drivers are the face of our business and their interaction with our customers is crucial to our success. In this role you will be delivering frozen Wiltshire Farm Foods meals and bringing sunshine to our customers doors (no matter what the weather is doing). As such, you will enjoy meeting people and always be willing to go above and beyond what is expected. Along with this, you will be working within our coldstore that runs at a chilly -20ºc. Protective clothing designed to deal with this temperature will be provided along with breaks to warm up and have a hot drink when necessary. You will need to work as part of the wider team always looking to help out the office team. We encourage movement between teams to provide flexibility, job satisfaction, personal development and knowledge of the whole business. We’re passionate about Making a Real Difference to people’s lives with delicious and nutritious meals for everyone to enjoy. View our latest TV ad here: https://www.wiltshirefarmfoods.com/blog/our-new-tv-ad-launches £13.05 per hour (£13.45 per hour from 1st May 2026) + bonus + company benefits.

jobs.title: Ingénieur Sécurité Cloud GCP ou AWS F/H
jobs.description: Dans le cadre de la mise en place d’un Centre de Compétences « Cloud Center of Excellence » pour notre client, nous recherchons un(e) Ingénieur Sécurité Cloud. Vous serez amenés à intervenir sur les sujets suivants : - Accompagner les équipes informatiques dans les pratiques de « sécurité dès la conception », visant à mettre en œuvre des politiques et des modèles de sécurité tout au long du cycle de vie des applications, de la conception à l'exécution • Maîtriser et mettre en place les principes du « Zero Trust Architecture ». • Maîtriser les services AWS ou GCP relatifs à la sécurité. • Garantir la prise de décisions de sécurité judicieuses, conformément aux politiques de l'entreprise. • Etre certifié o AWS Security speciality o ou GCP Security Engineer Ce poste situé à Guyancourt (78) Chez Orange, seules vos compétences comptent : Quel que soit votre âge, genre, origine, religion, orientation sexuelle, neuroatypie, handicap ou apparence, nous encourageons la diversité au sein de nos équipes car c’est une force pour le collectif et un vecteur d’innovation Nous sommes une entreprise handi-accueillante : n'hésitez pas à nous faire part de vos besoins spécifiques Orange obtient la certification Top Employer Global 2021. Cette certification consacre les meilleures politiques et pratiques en termes de programmes de ressources humaines. Le Groupe est certifié « Top Employer » depuis maintenant 12 ans.

jobs.title: Business Manager Performance Energétique F/H
jobs.description: Notre entité, intégrée au sein d’Orange Business, dispose de compétences pointues et reconnues dans l'intégration de solutions globales en Connectivité (LAN/WIFI/WAN/SDWAN/Data Center/MPN), Workplace (O365/Teams, Cloud Telephony, Digital Building, Mobilité) & IoT auprès de clients grands comptes et entreprises. Son rôle est de simplifier la vie digitale de ses clients, en leur offrant les meilleures expériences collaboratives tout en garantissant la meilleure connectivité grâce à des offres sur mesure. La Practice Performance Energétique et Développement Durable a pour mission de développer des offres de services et de technologies destinées à optimiser les consommations d’énergie et réduire l’empreinte carbone de ses clients. Missions : Les missions confiées au Business Manager sont organisées autour de trois grands axes : les actions commerciales, l’expertise et l’animation marketing. Définir et déployer les actions commerciales Identifier des cibles clients prioritaires et établir des listes de prospects Définir les méthodes et les objectifs des plans de prospection Contribuer activement à ces plans par des actions de prospection directe Identifier et animer les relais commerciaux internes (KAM, AM, BM, etc.) Alimenter et mettre à jour les outils de suivi commercial (CRM, fichier de suivi pipe) et assurer le reporting auprès de la direction et des parties prenantes Développer et porter l’expertise métier Apporter l’expertise métier tant en interne qu’auprès des prospects et clients Préparer et participer aux rendez-vous organisés par les commerciaux pour aider à qualifier le besoin et les réponses adéquates Assurer une veille globale (concurrence, marché, réglementation, acteurs, etc.) Détection d'opportunité de nouvelles solutions à intégrer dans le portefeuille de solutions ou partenariats à développer Définir les actions marketing et appuyer leur réalisation Appui à la réalisation d'outils de communication (supports marketing et commercial, références clients, campagne e-mailing, etc.) Proposition et appui à la réalisation de campagnes marketing opérationnel Aide à la préparation et participation aux salons d'affaires, webinaires et évènements divers Le Business Manager est assisté dans sa mission par 2 alternants qui l’appuient sur les plans d’action commerciaux et marketing.

jobs.title: Digital Marketing Manager
jobs.description: Fixed term contract for 12 months Join our rapidly growing business in a role as Digital Marketing Manager (FTC) focusing primarily on the digital marketing and paid digital strategies to support B2B’s commercial growth, as well as managing the apetito.co.uk website strategy. As a digital marketing specialist, your role will be pivotal in enhancing our online presence. Reporting to the Head of Proposition & Marketing, you will play a key role in contributing to the company’s ambitious growth targets and driving our online brand visibility. This is a fantastic opportunity for a collaborative, analytical and creative marketeer to work within a supportive, experienced and passionate team. This role is based in Trowbridge and you will be required to work on-site at least 3 days per week.

jobs.title: Technicien de production H/F
jobs.description: Vous êtes passionné(e) par la cybersécurité et souhaitez évoluer dans un environnement innovant en pleine expansion ? En mission interne et dans le cadre de l'expansion du Global Security Operations Center (GSOC) du groupe Orange, vous interviendrez au sein de la Direction de la Sécurité (DSEC) pour piloter des projets d'intégration de solutions de cybersécurité. Votre rôle sera essentiel pour assurer la supervision et la protection des actifs critiques du groupe. Vos missions : Piloter des projets d’intégration de solutions de cybersécurité : suivre le déploiement des solutions de sécurité et/ou mise en place de la supervision. Collaborer avec des équipes pluridisciplinaires : travailler main dans la main avec nos experts techniques, d'autres entités du groupe, équipes d’intégration et d’exploitation ainsi que celles du GSOC pour garantir la mise en œuvre des solutions adaptées. Évaluer et synthétiser les besoins : recueillir les besoins et synthétiser l'information, rédiger des comptes-rendus, élaborer des plannings, préparer des présentations pour le comité de pilotage, réaliser des schémas d’architecture haut niveau.

jobs.title: Ingénieur Support Sécurité H/F
jobs.description: Orange International Networks Infrastructures & Services (OINIS) est une direction technique du Groupe Orange, déployée à l’échelle mondiale avec plus de 1400 collaborateurs hautement qualifiés. Notre mission est de concevoir, planifier, maintenir et sécuriser les réseaux internationaux et services associés pour l’ensemble du groupe. Créée en 2021, OINIS SEC a pour objectif de protéger l’ensemble des infrastructures d’OINIS, depuis l’ingénierie des solutions jusqu’à la supervision de sécurité, en passant par le déploiement. Elle se structure autour de deux pôles principaux : expertise/anticipation et développement/support. En intégrant l’équipe ENG (Engineering), composée de 12 collaborateurs répartis en France, Maroc et Inde, vos missions principales seont les suivantes : Définition, l’industrialisation et le support des outils de sécurité (contrôle d’accès, authentification, gestion des vulnérabilités, supervision). Déploiement et le maintien en conditions opérationnelles et de sécurité des équipements réseaux. Mise en place des règles de filtrage Firewall. Conception d’architectures et solutions pour la protection des infrastructures, en comprenant les besoins clients et en sélectionnant les composants adaptés. Collaboration étroite avec les équipes Network et TelcoCloud pour le déploiement et la gestion des solutions. Promotion des solutions open source, tout en utilisant des produits commerciaux si nécessaire. Rédaction de documents d’architecture, guides de bonnes pratiques et référentiels. Production, configuration et déploiement des équipements réseaux IP. Support aux équipes opérationnelles pour la gestion et la résolution des incidents. Intégration de l’automatisation dans le déploiement des solutions de sécurité. Gestion de projets pour déployer les solutions dans le respect des plannings et contraintes financières.

jobs.title: Expert IoT et Réseaux H/F
jobs.description: Rejoignez une équipe dynamique au sein d'Orange, leader dans le domaine des technologies de communication. Nous sommes engagés à offrir des solutions innovantes et responsables qui répondent aux besoins de nos clients dans un monde en constante évolution. Notre culture d'entreprise valorise la collaboration, la créativité et l'exploration, tout en plaçant l'humain au cœur de nos préoccupations. Prêt(e) à connecter le futur ? Rejoignez-nous et devenez l'architecte des solutions IoT ! Description des missions : Vous concevrez et développerez des solutions IoT innovantes en intégrant des technologies réseau avancées. Vous intégrerez des modules logiciels de partenaires et fournisseurs tiers pour enrichir nos solutions. Vous rédigerez des spécifications techniques et fonctionnelles claires et précises pour guider le développement et/ou réaliserez des mission de conseil ou d’audit auprès de nos clients. Vous contribuerez au développement de bout en bout d’applications (front et back) et à leur test déploiement chez nos clients industriels. Vous accompagnerez nos clients dans la validation métier de la solution, en veillant à son bon fonctionnement dans l'environnement de production. Vous assurerez le suivi et l'optimisation des performances des solutions déployées sur le terrain. Vous participerez à des études et projets d'innovation pour anticiper les évolutions du marché et des technologies. Vous serez en charge de la formation et du support technique pour les équipes internes et les clients. Vous collaborerez avec des équipes pluridisciplinaires pour définir et mettre en œuvre des architectures IoT robustes. Vous apporterez votre expertise en support à l'avant-vente pour aider à la définition des besoins clients et à la proposition de solutions adaptées.

jobs.title: Ingénieur Devops
jobs.description: Intégré à une équipe de 21 personnes composés d'ingénieurs DevOps, Ingénieurs SecOps, Administrateurs Systèmes et de Chefs de projet ; vos missions seront les suivantes : Participer aux travaux BUILD/RUN sur le socle H2S : Gestion de l’obsolescence des composants, couche OS (TalosOS), composants Kube (Secret reloader, Cilium, ….) et applicatifs (ArgoCD, Prometheus, Graylog, ….) Evolution de l’architecture sur les nouvelles fonctionnalités requises Mettre en place de la CI/CD pour nos produits métier et nos services socles. Rédaction des pipelines, intégration outils tiers comme pour le scan sécurité, amélioration des templates, debug de l’existant Participer aux travaux de BUILD/RUN sur le tooling DevOps associé notamment Gitlab, Runners, Repositories,…. Gestion des runners et leur automatisation en IAC MCO/MCS Gitlab et repositories, AWX, …. Accompagner les équipes de développement à l’intégration de leurs applications sur nos socles en particulier H2S : charts Helm, ArgoCD, playbook ansible Co-écriture des pipelines CI/CD et playbook ansible avec les équipes de dev, intégration ArgoCD et Kubernetes ou sur infrastructure legacy, assistance à la création d’images spécifiques à partir du catalogue Participer aux guidelines/Patterns DevOps : templates CI/Charts, bonnes pratiques, security by design… Amélioration de la base de templates CI/CD Formation des devs/lead devs Participation aux travaux d’architecture R&D des produits et services

jobs.title: Ingénieur Qualité et Affaires Règlementaires
jobs.description: Assurer le rôle de PCVRR (personne chargée de veiller au respect de la réglementation) Assurer le rôle de correspondant local de matériovigilance (CLMV) Assurer le rôle de Référent Qualité pour les processus opérationnels Assurer la veille normative et règlementaire et la réponse aux exigences de notre organisation Assurer la diffusion du SMI (Système de Management Intégré) dans l’intranet de l’entreprise

jobs.title: Ingénieur(e) DevSecOps F/H
jobs.description: Description de l'entité Vous rejoignez l’équipe AOI, un groupe dynamique et engagé au sein de la Business Line Defense Center d’Orange. Cette équipe joue un rôle clé dans la sécurisation et l’innovation des solutions technologiques dédiées à la défense, en alliant expertise technique et esprit collaboratif pour relever les défis de demain. Description des missions DevSecOps : quand la sécurité rencontre l’automatisation, ça donne votre prochain défi ! Vos missions quotidiennes Vous prenez en charge la finalisation, le développement et la maintenance de la solution Qevlar AI, un outil d’investigation automatique des incidents de sécurité basé sur l’intelligence artificielle, déployé dans un environnement GCP et Kubernetes. Vous assurez la conception, le déploiement et la maintenance des pipelines CI/CD sécurisés pour garantir la qualité et la sécurité des livrables. Vous collaborez étroitement avec les équipes de développement et de sécurité pour intégrer les meilleures pratiques DevSecOps. Vous automatisez les processus de sécurité et de conformité tout au long du cycle de vie des applications. Vous surveillez et analysez les incidents de sécurité pour proposer des améliorations continues. Vous participez à la définition et à la mise en œuvre des politiques de sécurité dans un environnement cloud et on-premise. Vous pourrez être amené à travailler sur nos solutions d’orchestration, principalement XSOAR et N8N. Les compétences que vous aurez l’opportunité de développer grâce à ce poste Ce poste vous permettra de renforcer vos compétences techniques en DevSecOps, notamment dans l’intégration de solutions d’intelligence artificielle pour la sécurité, la gestion avancée des environnements cloud et l’automatisation des processus. Vous développerez également votre capacité à travailler dans un contexte exigeant et innovant, au sein d’une équipe collaborative et engagée.

jobs.title: Senior PR Executive
jobs.description: As Senior PR Executive, you will play a key role in delivering high-quality, impactful PR activity that strengthens apetito’s reputation across our priority markets. You will need a high degree of autonomy and judgement, and be trusted to lead the delivery of high-profile PR activity under the direction of the PR Manager. Sitting within the central Brand & Communications team, you will work closely with colleagues across the business and external partners to bring our stories to life, ensuring our voice is credible, consistent and values-led. You will also act as a role model within the PR team, helping to maintain high standards and support the development of others. Most importantly, you will help share how apetito and Wiltshire Farm Foods are making a real difference to customers, communities and colleagues every single day. At apetito | Wiltshire Farm Foods, we are on an ambitious journey. With bold growth targets ahead, how we tell our story and protect our reputation will play a critical role in helping us succeed. We are looking for someone with experience in PR and media relations, able to craft compelling stories as well as demonstrate the commercial impact of PR delivery. This role is based in our Trowbridge head office and you will need to be on site at least 3 days per week.

jobs.title: Ingénieur exploitation client F/H
jobs.description: Le poste de Security Operations Engineer est intégré au sein d’une équipe mutalisée. Il aura comme missions principales de : Garantir le bon fonctionnement des solutions opérationnelles des clients Prendre en charge les demandes clients et la résolution des incidents techniques complexes (niveau 2 / 3) Être un atout majeur sur des environnements de sécurité périmètriques, en accompagnant la montée en compétences de l’ensemble de l’équipe sur cette technologie En tant que tel, vous prendrez la responsabilité des tâches suivantes : Traiter les demandes clients : changements et/ou incidents complexes Identifier les problèmes et analyser leurs causes afin de proposer et mettre œuvre un plan d’actions correctif Gérer les escalades avec les clients et partenaires Maintenir la documentation client et rédiger les procédures d’exploitation

jobs.title: Ingénieur Développeur Confirmé
jobs.description: Conception Participation aux réunions fonctionnelles avec le Product Owner et autres parties prenantes Garantie de l'homogénéité des développements et de l'architecture en relation avec les autres équipes de la R&D Réalisation des spécifications techniques et participation aux analyses conceptuelles Chiffrage des charges de développement Développement Réalisation du développement, qualité de code Implémentation des tests unitaires Référent auprès que QA intégré à l’équipe de dev Participation aux différentes cérémonies d’un sprint Scrum : Daily Meeting, Sprint Review, etc. Production de la documentation technique du logiciel Rédaction et vérification des manuels d'utilisation et d'installation Reporting (dérives, problèmes techniques, ...) à l’équipe et autres parties prenantes Divers Formation et assistance aux services tels que : pôles expertise, intégration et support Assure la veille technologique Coordination entre l’équipe de développement et le Product Owner Reporting régulier de l'activité au Manager

jobs.title: Alternant consultant Transformation Organisationnelle F/H
jobs.description: Digital Services est l' ESN d'Orange Business qui combine les expertises Cloud, Digital, Data et IA. Plus de 4 000 experts répartis dans toute la France accompagnent des entreprises et institutions publiques dans leur transformation numérique. Nous rejoindre, c'est participer à l'accompagnement de nos clients à travers des missions stimulantes et variées. 🟨 Au sein de l’équipe Design vous accompagnerez l’équipe sur des problématiques de transformation organisationnelle, d’évolution des modèles de fonctionnement et d’amélioration de l’expérience collaborateurs et clients. 🟨 Vos missions seront les suivantes : - Contribution aux projets de transformation • Participer aux phases de cadrage : compréhension des enjeux stratégiques, analyse de l’existant, identification des irritants organisationnels • Réaliser des diagnostics (parcours, processus, interactions, gouvernance, modes de collaboration) • Contribuer à la modélisation de situations actuelles et cibles (blueprints, operating models simplifiés, chaînes de valeur) • Aider à la formulation de recommandations structurantes et actionnables - Facilitation et intelligence collective • Participer à la conception et à l’animation d’ateliers avec des parties prenantes variées (métiers, fonctions support, direction…) • Produire des supports favorisant l’alignement et la prise de décision • Accompagner les dynamiques de co-construction - Structuration d’une offre de transformation • Contribuer à la formalisation de notre proposition de valeur • Réaliser des benchmarks sur les tendances en transformation des organisations • Participer à la création de supports commerciaux et méthodologiques • Aider à clarifier nos approches (cadres d’intervention, livrables, narration) - Développement de la posture de consultant • Apprendre à naviguer dans des environnements complexes • Développer une pensée structurée et systémique • Renforcer sa capacité à problématiser • Gagner en impact à l’oral comme à l’écrit

jobs.title: Alternant Business Manager F/H
jobs.description: Digital Services est l' ESN d'Orange Business qui combine les expertises Cloud, Digital, Data et IA. Plus de 4 000 experts répartis dans toute la France accompagnent des entreprises et institutions publiques dans leur transformation numérique. Nous rejoindre, c'est participer à l'accompagnement de nos clients à travers des missions stimulantes et variées. 🟨Nous recherchons un(e) alternant(e) Business Manager pour notre équipe nantaise. Rattaché(e) à l’une de nos agences sectorielles : Banque Assurance, dans une équipe dynamique et expérimentée, vous intervenez dans le cadre de projets de transformation digitale, en tant que développeur commercial, auprès de prospects et client privés ou publics sur les territoires Grand-Ouest et Grands Comptes Nationaux. 🟨Dans le cadre du développement de nos offres de prestation de service IT en région & de la solution DXP Liferay au niveau national, vos missions seront les suivantes : Participer à l’élaboration de la stratégie de développement du CA sur votre périmètre Analyser les métiers et l'environnement de vos clients : stratégie, écosystème, interlocuteurs stratégiques (DG, DSI, RH, marketing, commercial), en identifiant les enjeux du client et leurs impacts sur leurs activités Démarcher nos prospects et clients dans la région et sur des grands comptes nationaux Proposer nos solutions (Produit et service) dans le but de générer des leads commerciaux Suivre le pipeline commercial et son développement marketing avec l’Editeur Animer les leads et accompagner les vendeurs sur les dernières briques technologiques Participer activement à l'animation commerciale interne et externe.

jobs.title: Quality & Food Safety Auditor
jobs.description: The technical department can be described as the ‘Conscience of the Business’ with a core remit of keeping the business informed to ensure strong strategic and tactical decision, provide customer and supplier confidence in relation to food safety and quality standards and to safeguard the business by maintaining a strong due diligence defense. This is achieved by providing assurance that legal, customer, supplier and business standards of food quality and safety are maintained, ensuring procedural compliance, understanding and effectiveness and driving remedial actions to resolve the root cause of any issues and prevent recurrence. Additionally, the department has a responsibility to drive continuous improvement as an outcome of the core remit by setting standards and keeping abreast of developments that may impact the food industry. This role is working 36 hours per week, Friday - Sunday, 6am-6pm.

jobs.title: Technical Compliance  Manager
jobs.description: At apetito, we are committed to delivering the highest standards of food safety, quality and compliance. We’re looking for an experienced Technical Compliance Manager to help us maintain and continually improve these standards. As Technical Compliance Manager, you will lead the validation, verification and audit of our UK food safety and quality systems. You’ll work closely with technical and operational teams to ensure full compliance with legal requirements and maintain our AA grade BRCGS Food Safety certification. This is a fantastic time to join us. As we grow towards £300m in turnover, we’re investing in a new, state‑of‑the‑art frozen food manufacturing facility alongside our existing site in Trowbridge. Your responsibilities will cover both sites, offering significant opportunity for development as our operations expand.

jobs.title: Sr Editorial Writer
jobs.description: Are you a sharp, adaptable writer who can transform complex ideas into clear, compelling narratives? Do you believe great writing can shape perception, strengthen a brand, and build meaningful connections with audiences? HGA is looking for an Editorial & Brand Writer to help bring our voice to life across channels, audiences, and markets. As part of HGA’s Digital Marketing & Content Strategy team, this role plays a central part in defining how we tell our story as a national interdisciplinary design firm. You’ll craft, refine, and elevate content that advances brand visibility, market positioning, business development, and strategic firm initiatives. From thought leadership and executive communications to digital content, campaign copy, and stories about our work and people, you’ll help ensure everything we publish is clear, credible, and unmistakably HGA. The ideal candidate is both a strong writer and a disciplined editor—someone who can generate original ideas, collaborate with subject matter experts, and maintain a consistent brand voice across diverse formats. Curiosity, strategic thinking, and audience awareness are essential, as is the ability to translate complex concepts from architecture, engineering, planning, interiors, research, and innovation into engaging, accessible narratives. You'll thrive at HGA if you ... Are energized by turning complex ideas into clear, compelling stories Can shape and refine content without losing the original voice behind it Have a strong instinct for tone, voice, and narrative Stay organized and thoughtful in a fast-paced, deadline-driven environment Enjoy connecting storytelling to strategy, brand, and business goals In this role you will: Thought Leadership & Storytelling Develop and write multi-format content including articles, insights, project stories, executive perspectives, video scripts, and campaign narratives. Translate complex design, technical, and market-specific ideas into clear, compelling, and commercially relevant messaging. Identify and shape timely themes across HGA's eight markets to drive proactive storytelling. Partner with firm leaders to elevate executive voice and external visibility. Editorial Excellence & Brand Voice Serve as a steward of HGA's brand voice, ensuring consistency, clarity, and quality across all content. Edit and refine content from subject matter experts and contributors across the firm. Establish and evolve editorial standards, frameworks, and storytelling approaches. Champion the audience perspective, ensuring content is engaging, relevant, and easy to understand. Content Strategy & Campaign Integration Collaborate with digital, PR, and marketing teams to develop integrated content across campaigns and channels. Adapt and repurpose content for web, social, email, video, and presentations. Contribute to editorial calendars aligned to market priorities and firm initiatives. Support strategic initiatives including brand evolution, website experience, and awareness campaigns. Collaboration & Workflow Partner cross-functionally with market sector leaders, designers, digital strategists, and PR Lead or support content development workflows (briefs, interviews, outlines, reviews) Ensure alignment across stakeholders while maintaining efficiency and momentum Utilize tools and platforms (e.g., CMS, HubSpot, DAM) to manage and publish content Performance & Optimization Leverage insights, analytics, and feedback to continuously improve content effectiveness. Identify opportunities for new content based on performance, trends, and business needs. Contribute to building a scalable, measurable content ecosystem. What you will need to have: Bachelor’s degree in English, Journalism, Communications, Marketing, or a related field, or equivalent experience. 8-10+ years of experience in editorial, content strategy, or brand writing, ideally in professional services, AEC, or similarly complex industries. Exceptional writing and editing skills, with the ability to move seamlessly between big-picture narrative and detail-oriented execution. Proven ability to translate complex concepts into clear, engaging content. Experience developing thought leadership and executive-level communications. Strong collaboration and stakeholder management skills across diverse teams. Comfort working across formats: long-form, short-form, digital, video, and social. A strategic mindset with the ability to connect content to business goals and audience needs. Familiarity with digital platforms, content systems, and performance metrics. Salary Information: The pay range listed aligns to the geography of the office for which this position is posted. Actual compensation will be based upon work experience, education, licensure requirements and/or skill level and will be finalized at the time of offer. Individuals in this role working more than 24 hours a week on a regular basis are eligible to participate in HGA’s robust benefit programs which include an annual bonus and success sharing bonus program, 401(k) guaranteed contribution, paid parental leave, medical, dental and life insurance plans as well as PTO and short- and long-term disability programs. Salary Range: $104,100 - 123,300 (Mid-West) or $110,800 - $133,200 (East and West Coast) depending on qualifications and experience. Additional Information: At HGA, you’ll do the best work of your career. We design with impact by diving deep into the people and passions that shape every place. Empathy fuels us, curiosity drives us, and meaningful work leads to innovation. We celebrate individuality, embrace flexibility, and thrive together in the office three days a week. Join our nationally recognized, award-winning team of architects and engineers—and help us shape what’s next. Learn more about us here . Application Requirements & Instructions Please submit resume for consideration.

jobs.title: Sr Public Relations Manager
jobs.description: Shape the Stories Behind Award-Winning Design – Join HGA as a Senior Public Relations Manager ! As a national leader in architecture, engineering, and design, HGA is recognized not only for the places we create—but for the ideas, insights, and impact that shape them. The Senior PR Manager is central to amplifying that voice on a national stage. In this role, you will lead the development and execution of HGA’s public relations strategy, elevating our brand, strengthening our market presence, and positioning our people, projects, and perspectives at the forefront of industry and cultural conversations. You’ll collaborate closely with firm leadership, marketing teams, and external partners to drive integrated, high‑impact visibility across markets and channels. This position reflects an expanded, modern vision of PR—one that goes beyond traditional media relations to build credibility, discoverability, and influence. You will partner directly with clients to amplify shared stories, extend HGA’s reach through their platforms and audiences, and navigate an evolving landscape shaped by AI, search, and cross‑channel content ecosystems. You'll t hrive  at HGA if you... Think beyond traditional PR and are excited to shape how a brand shows up across channels, platforms, and partnerships Are energized by turning complex ideas into clear, compelling stories that resonate with multiple audiences Can operate at both a strategic and executional level—connecting big-picture thinking to day-to-day action Build strong relationships easily, from journalists to clients to internal leaders Are curious about how media, content, and technology are evolving—and eager to adapt and lead through that change Enjoy working across disciplines and bringing people together around a shared narrative and purpose In this role you will: Strategic Leadership & Planning Lead the development and execution of HGA’s firmwide PR strategy, aligned with brand, marketing, and business priorities. Define and oversee annual PR plans and integrated campaigns across markets and sectors. Establish KPIs and measurement frameworks to track brand awareness, media impact, and campaign performance. Monitor and translate emerging trends (media, AI/search, content distribution) into actionable strategy. Partner with firm leadership to shape narratives and elevate executive visibility. Media Relations & Campaign Execution Oversee proactive and reactive media strategy across national, regional, and trade outlets. Build and maintain strong relationships with key journalists, editors, and industry influencers. Lead development of press materials, messaging frameworks, and story pitches. Drive high-impact PR campaigns tied to firm initiatives, priority projects, and market growth areas. Client Partnership & External Amplification Partner with clients as an extension of their teams to shape and promote shared project stories and impact. Identify and execute co-marketing and cross-promotion opportunities that elevate both HGA and client brands. jobs.title: Customer Delivery Driver/Picker
jobs.description: This position is working 40 hours per week, any 5 in 6 days including bank holidays. Our drivers are the face of our business and their interaction with our customers is crucial to our success. In this role you will be delivering frozen Wiltshire Farm Foods meals and bringing sunshine to our customers doors (no matter what the weather is doing). As such, you will enjoy meeting people and always be willing to go above and beyond what is expected. Along with this, you will be working within our coldstore that runs at a chilly -20ºc. Protective clothing designed to deal with this temperature will be provided along with breaks to warm up and have a hot drink when necessary. You will need to work as part of the wider team always looking to help out the office team. We encourage movement between teams to provide flexibility, job satisfaction, personal development and knowledge of the whole business. We’re passionate about Making a Real Difference to people’s lives with delicious and nutritious meals for everyone to enjoy. View our latest TV ad here: https://www.wiltshirefarmfoods.com/blog/our-new-tv-ad-launches £13.05 per hour(£13.45 per hour from 1 May 2026) + bonus + company benefits.

jobs.description: Technical Manager – Be at the Heart of a Major New Investment Join apetito at a transformational moment in our journey. Having exceeded £260m turnover, we are now investing over £100m in building a state‑of‑the‑art manufacturing facility —a major milestone that will support our continued growth and innovation in food manufacturing. As our new Technical Manager, you’ll play a pivotal role not only in leading food safety and quality during the operational phase, but also in shaping the future of the site as it comes to life. This includes valuable involvement throughout the installation and commissioning of the new facility—an opportunity few roles can offer. What You’ll Be Doing While construction is underway, you’ll be based in our existing kitchen, located just a short walk from the new site. Reporting to the Group Technical Manager, you will: Work closely with the current Site Technical Manager to support the team with a strong focus on systems, governance, and compliance Build a deep understanding of our complex operation—managing hundreds of ingredients and materials that go into producing hundreds of delicious, nutritious recipes Begin partnering with the project team as the new build progresses, ensuring that food safety and quality standards are fully embedded throughout installation and commissioning Recruit, develop, and inspire your new team, building strong relationships across the site Design and implement robust quality systems to support the new kitchen’s launch Ensure we achieve food registration and secure an AA grade against the BRCGS Global Standard for Food Safety at the earliest opportunity

jobs.title: Customer Service Advisor
jobs.description: This role is working 30 hours per week, any 4 in 6 days The role of our Customer Services Advisor is critical to the success of our business. Our customers are predominantly elderly and rely upon our office teams to take their orders over the phone or via the internet. The defining characteristics of our Customer Services Advisors are that they’re organised and efficient. However, apart from the technical skills that are required with the role, the most important attributes are that we demonstrate empathy, patience, kindness, friendliness, care and warmth to all our customers. The role involves the taking of customer orders and also the making of outbound courtesy calls. These calls are particularly important as many customers live alone and the phone calls they receive are often the only contact they may have in a single day. As such, many customers become very attached to their “friends” at Wiltshire Farm Foods. Wiltshire Farm Foods supplies frozen meal solutions direct to the consumer. We have a cold store to accommodate the stock necessary to be able to be able to deliver to our customers each week. The ultimate goal is to provide a service to our customers that exceed expectations while at the same time continuously improving efficiencies. £13.05 per hour (£13.45 per hour from 1st May 2026) + bonus + company benefits.

jobs.title: Registered Nurse (RN), Variable Part-Time Day Shift,  PACU
jobs.description: Shady Grove Medical Center If you are a current Adventist HealthCare employee, please click this link to apply through your Workday account. Shady Grove Medical Center seeks to hire a Registered Nurse who will embrace our mission to extend God’s care through the ministry of physical, mental and spiritual healing. As a PACU Registered Nurse, you will: • Educate patients and family through the continuum of care while also striving to advance their own knowledge and skills through careful consideration of feedback and guidance provided by more senior nurses and the Clinical Nurse Manager. • Serve as role models to Level I nurses and use electronic resources to support best practices in patient care. • Demonstrate professionalism, adheres to ANA standards of practice, and exemplifies the Adventist HealthCare values as they fulfill our nursing mission and vision. • Use equipment and technology correctly, to delegate non-nursing duties appropriately, and to maximize efficiency and effectiveness • Develop a plan of care according to patient population. • Identify impending changes in patient condition accurately and initiates corrective action. • Mentor students, colleagues and team members in patient-centered care. • Conduct purposeful rounding from patient and family perspective. • Conduct efficient literature searches and applies findings appropriately to practice. • Identify opportunities for improvement in clinical care and participates in change of practice based on evidence. Qualifications include: • Graduate of an accredited nursing program • Passing score on NCLEX • Minimum 1 year of RN experience required. PACU Registered Nurse experience or related acute care experience preferred • Current Maryland State nursing license • Active American Heart Association Basic Life Support (BLS) certification required • Active American Heart Association (ACLS) certification required • Active American Heart Association (PALS) certification required Work Schedule: Employment Type: Variable Part-time Hours per Week: 8 hrs/week Typical Daily Schedule: Varied hours Shift Type: Day Weekend Requirements: no Holiday Requirements: Required/On-call/Follow company holiday calendar On-Call: no Shift Differential Eligible: Yes Pay Range: $37.00 - $55.00 If the salary range is not displayed or if the position is Per Diem (with a fixed rate), salary discussions will take place during the screening process. Under the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA), this position is classified as: United States of America (Non-Exempt) At Adventist HealthCare our job is to care for you. We do this by offering: Work life balance through nonrotating shifts Recognition and rewards for professional expertise 403(b) retirement plan Free Employee parking Employee Assistance Program (EAP) support As a faith-based organization, with over a century of caring for the communities in the Maryland area, Adventist HealthCare has earned a reputation for high-quality, compassionate care. Adventist HealthCare was the first and is the largest healthcare provider in Montgomery County. If you want to make a difference in someone’s life every day, consider a position with a team of professionals who are doing just that, making a difference. Join the Adventist HealthCare team today, apply now to be considered! COVID-19 Vaccination Adventist HealthCare strongly recommends all applicants to be fully vaccinated for COVID-19 before commencing employment. Applicants may be required to furnish proof of vaccination. Tobacco and Drug Statement Tobacco use is a well-recognized preventable cause of death in the United States and an important public health issue. In order to promote and maintain a healthy work environment, Adventist HealthCare will not hire applicants for employment who either state that they are nicotine users or who test positive for nicotine and drug use. While some jurisdictions, including Maryland, permit the use of marijuana for medical purposes, marijuana continues to be classified as an illegal drug under the federal Controlled Substances Act.  As a result, medical marijuana use will not be accepted as a valid explanation for a positive drug test result. Adventist HealthCare will withdraw offers of employment to applicants who test positive for Cotinine (nicotine) and marijuana. Those testing positive are given the opportunity to re-apply in 90 days, if they can truthfully attest that they have not used any nicotine products in the past ninety (90) days and successfully pass follow-up testing. ("Nicotine products" include, but are not limited to: cigarettes, cigars, pipes, chewing tobacco, e-cigarettes, vaping products, hookah, and nicotine replacement products (e.g., nicotine gum, nicotine patches, nicotine lozenges, etc.). Equal Employment Opportunity Adventist HealthCare is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer. We are committed to attracting, engaging, and developing the best people to cultivate our mission-centric culture. Our goal is to have a welcoming, equitable, and safe place to work and grow for all employees, no matter their background. AHC does not discriminate in employment opportunities or practices on the basis of race, ethnicity, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity, pregnancy and related medical conditions, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Adventist HealthCare will make reasonable accommodations for applicants with disabilities, in accordance with applicable law.  Adventist HealthCare is a religious organization as defined under applicable law; however, it will endeavor to provide reasonable accommodations for applicants’ religious beliefs. Applicants who wish to request accommodations for disabilities or religious belief should contact the Support Center HR Office.

Practice Details Full-time employment with MemorialCare Medical Group Strong cardiology program and a network of over 200 primary care providers Award-winning heart program with full support of the cardiologists and administration Total Rewards Employed position with MemorialCare Medical Group Competitive salary with incentive compensation Eligible to be considered for Shareholder/Partner status with MCMG after two years of full-time employment Full and comprehensive benefits for the physician and family 401(k) retirement plan with employer matching, 529 College Savings Plan, Continuing Medical Education reimbursement fund, malpractice insurance, and complimentary personal financial planning services Generous paid time off program About MemorialCare We’re a nonprofit integrated health system that includes leading hospitals – Long Beach Medical Center, Miller Children's & Women's Hospital Long Beach, Orange Coast Medical Center, and Saddleback Medical Center; award-winning medical groups – MemorialCare Medical Group and Greater Newport Physicians; Select Health Plan; and convenient outpatient health centers, urgent care centers, imaging centers, breast centers, surgical centers, physical therapy centers and dialysis centers throughout Orange and Los Angeles Counties. About Long Beach and Southern California: Located on the coast of the Pacific Ocean south of Los Angeles and just west of Orange County, Long Beach is the sixth largest city in the State of California. Offering all the world class amenities of a large metropolitan city, coupled with its strong sense of community and pride, Long Beach is one of the most vibrant communities in the country. The city has been referred to as the "Most Diverse City" in the country by USA Today and one of the "Best Communities for Young People" two years in a row. With its ideal location in Southern California, year-round comfortable climate, healthy business environment and many cultural pursuits, the city is alive with activity. Long Beach is home to an abundance of cultural and recreational options. Expansive beaches, three marinas, five golf courses, the Aquarium of the Pacific, and the annual Grand Prix of Long Beach serve to draw 6.5 million visitors a year. Long Beach has easy access to all the amenities and attractions of Los Angeles, Orange, and San Bernardino counties, including professional sporting events, music festivals, gourmet dining, film and television studios, Disneyland, and snow skiing in the local mountains.

jobs.title: Customer Delivery Driver/Picker
jobs.description: This position is working 24 hours per week, any 3 in 6 days including bank holidays. Our drivers are the face of our business and their interaction with our customers is crucial to our success. In this role you will be delivering frozen Wiltshire Farm Foods meals and bringing sunshine to our customers doors (no matter what the weather is doing). As such, you will enjoy meeting people and always be willing to go above and beyond what is expected. Along with this, you will be working within our coldstore that runs at a chilly -20ºc. Protective clothing designed to deal with this temperature will be provided along with breaks to warm up and have a hot drink when necessary. You will need to work as part of the wider team always looking to help out the office team. We encourage movement between teams to provide flexibility, job satisfaction, personal development and knowledge of the whole business. We’re passionate about Making a Real Difference to people’s lives with delicious and nutritious meals for everyone to enjoy. View our latest TV ad here: https://www.wiltshirefarmfoods.com/blog/our-new-tv-ad-launches £13.05 per hour (£13.45 per hour from 1st May 2026) + bonus + company benefits.

jobs.description: Shape the future of healthcare design with HGA! Project Architect – Healthcare in Sacramento, San Francisco, Santa Monica or San Diego, CA HGA, a leading architectural, engineering, and planning firm, is seeking a talented and driven Project Architect to join our growing healthcare practice group in Northern and Southern California. We’re looking for someone with strong communication, presentation, time management, and interpersonal skills who thrives in a collaborative environment and is passionate about advancing healthcare design. In this role, you’ll work closely with Senior Designers, Project Managers, and other Project Architects to develop innovative and technically sound design solutions. You’ll lead the creation of detailed documentation while maintaining our firm’s high standards for design quality and client service. What You’ll Do: Lead the development of construction documents and ensure design intent is accurately carried through. Translate complex project goals into creative and functional design solutions. Collaborate across disciplines to coordinate architectural and engineering systems. Participate in client meetings and maintain strong relationships with key stakeholders. Ensure project milestones and deadlines are met with attention to detail and quality. Why HGA? Collaborative Culture: be part of a firm known for its integrated, cross-disciplinary teamwork and strong design ethos; Professional Growth: access to mentorship, continuing education, and leadership opportunities; and Meaningful Work: contribute to impactful healthcare projects that improve patient outcomes and shape the future of care in Calfornia. In this role you will: Design and u nderstand and contribute to the project's design philosophy. Provide major input in developing design and building performance strategies and convey these effectively to team members. Lead project teams to develop complete document sets based on the project phase. Provide technical design input and solutions to project challenges, supporting the overall design concept. Oversee technical design efforts, detailed design development, project coordination, and quality control during all project phases, collaborating closely with the Senior Designer. Execute applicable agency review analysis (accessibility, zoning, life-safety, etc.). Actively collaborate with and coordinate the work of consultants and knowledge resources to ensure project deliverables are coordinated, integrated, and support the design concept and performance goals. Set an example for team effort through the spirit of cooperation while directing, organizing, and mentoring junior staff. C ontribute to the preparation of design documents and lead the detailed development of construction documents during all project phases. Ensure complete, accurate, and coordinated project documentation. Oversee and perform construction administration duties, including reviewing shop drawings, material samples, RFI’s, RFP’s, and change orders for conformance with design. What you will need to have: Bachelor’s degree in Architecture California Architectural Licensure required LEED accreditation preferred 10+ years of experience, including work on small to large-scale projects. Understanding of the healthcare industry, especially in areas of clinical innovation, operational improvement, planning, and strategic positioning for complex hospital systems Deep understanding of healthcare programming, codes, and technical detailing Strong leadership and collaboration skills in team environments Proficiency in Revit required; experience with AutoCAD, SketchUp, Enscape, and other graphic tools desired. Expertise with project management software and Microsoft Office Suite products needed Excellent communication, presentation, and time management skills Experience with Lean and Evidence-based design is a plus. Experience with alternative delivery methods (IPD, Design Build, IFOA) preferred Salary Information: The pay range listed aligns to the geography of the office for which this position is posted. Actual compensation will be based upon work experience, education, licensure requirements and/or skill level and will be finalized at the time of offer. Individuals in this role working more than 24 hours a week on a regular basis are eligible to participate in HGA’s robust benefit programs which include an annual bonus and success sharing bonus program, 401(k) guaranteed contribution, paid parental leave, medical, dental and life insurance plans as well as PTO and short- and long-term disability programs. Salary Range: $108,200 - $130,000 depending on location (SAC, SF, LA or SD), qualifications and experience. Additional Information: At HGA, you’ll do the best work of your career. We design with impact by diving deep into the people and passions that shape every place. Empathy fuels us, curiosity drives us, and meaningful work leads to innovation. We celebrate individuality, embrace flexibility, and thrive together in the office three days a week. Join our nationally recognized, award-winning team of architects, engineers, and interior designers — help us shape what’s next. Learn more about us here . Application Requirements & Instructions Please submit resume and portfolio for consideration.

jobs.title: Project Manager
jobs.description: Join HGA and learn what passion for design really means in spaces that touch people's lives! Locations: Sacramento, San Fransisco, Santa Monica, or San Diego, CA Bring your experience and design expertise to an innovative architecture, engineering and interior design firm dedicated to designing exceptional spaces that inspire and transform communities. We are looking for a talented, design-sensitive, and ambitious Project Manager to join our California healthcare architecture teams and help shape the future of healthcare design. In this role, you will be an integral member of our project teams, contributing at every stage of the design process. Our practice encompasses a broad array of project types and scales, so versatility and adaptability to various collaborative design team settings are essential. Why HGA? Collaborative Culture: be part of a firm known for its integrated, cross-disciplinary teamwork and strong design ethos; Professional Growth: access to mentorship, continuing education, and leadership opportunities; and Meaningful Work: contribute to impactful projects that improve and shape the future architectural design. In this role you will: Collaborate with Principal in evaluation of project requirements and help to negotiate an acceptable owner-architect agreement including the fee, scope, and time schedule. Demonstrate task-focused mastery of project scope, schedule, budget and profitability. Complete projects on schedule, within budget and with maximum client satisfaction. Manage a program of multiple projects and associated client relationships effectively. Negotiate critical issues with Owner and Contractor as well as Architects, Engineers and Specialty Consultants within HGA and of other organizations and firms. Supervise the production of architectural construction documents for multiple small renovation projects. Lead a full-service architectural and engineering team, using past experience and technical knowledge to efficiently deliver quality documents, specifications, reports and cost estimates, keeping in mind schedules/budgets and provides coaching/communication as appropriate with team leaders and/or team members who are responsible for work flow/timelines, etc. Develop discipline cost and work-hour budgets for each project and monitor project costs, completion status against budget schedules/timelines. Oversee all correspondence and pertinent documents, making sure they are accurate; sign or obtain signatures and seek legal counsel as appropriate. Coordinate review and evaluation of contractor bids and work with Principal so that award recommendation can be submitted to owner. Provide day-to-day leadership in the absence of and as delegated by the Principal and/or Practice Group Leader keeping lines of communication open with all office management leaders and assist with preparation of client billings prior to submittal for signature. Collaborate with Practice Group Leader as requested in the marketing of projects including development of proposals and possible participation in client interviews. What you will need to have: Bachelor’s or master’s degree in architecture or related field Licensed professional preferred 8-10 years of project management experience honed on increasingly more complex projects Demonstrated skill in working on multiple smaller-sized to mid-sized, challenging projects simultaneously Exceptional interpersonal skills and the ability to communicate clearly with team members and clients and lead discussions on project budgets and management of project delivery Proficiency with industry accepted software and Microsoft Office products is needed, experience with Project Management software (Vision, Microsoft Project) is beneficial Salary Information: The pay range listed aligns to the geography of the office for which this position is posted. Actual compensation will be based upon work experience, education, licensure requirements and/or skill level and will be finalized at the time of offer. Individuals in this role working more than 24 hours a week on a regular basis are eligible to participate in HGA’s robust benefit programs which include an annual bonus and success sharing bonus program, 401(k) guaranteed contribution, paid parental leave, medical, dental and life insurance plans as well as PTO and short- and long-term disability programs. Salary Range: $107,000 - $131,200 depending on location (SAC, SF, LA or SD), qualifications and experience. Additional Information: At HGA, you’ll do the best work of your career. jobs.description: JOB DESCRIPTION: Acerca de Abbott Abbott es líder mundial en cuidado de la salud, que crea ciencia innovadora para mejorar la salud de las personas.  Siempre estamos mirando hacia el futuro, anticipando cambios en la ciencia y la tecnología médica. Trabajando en Abbott En Abbott, puedes hacer un trabajo que importa, crecer y aprender, cuidar de ti mismo y de tu familia, ser verdaderamente quien es y vivir una vida plena. Tendrás acceso a: Desarrollo profesional con una empresa internacional donde podrás hacer crecer la carrera que sueñas. Una compañía reconocida como mejor lugar para trabajar en docenas de países alrededor del mundo y nombrada una de las empresas más admiradas del mundo por Fortune. Una compañía que es reconocida como una de las mejores compañías grandes para trabajar, así como un mejor lugar para trabajar para la diversidad, las madres trabajadoras, mujeres ejecutivas y científicas. La Oportunidad Esta posición está ubicada en San Isidro. Nuestro negocio de nutrición desarrolla productos de nutrición basados en la ciencia para personas de todas las edades, desde ayudar a bebés y niños a crecer, hasta mantener los cuerpos adultos fuertes y activos. Millones de personas en todo el mundo cuentan con nuestras marcas líderes, incluidas Similac®, PediaSure®, Pedialyte®, Ensure®, Glucerna® y ZonePerfect®, para ayudar a obtener los nutrientes que necesitan para vivir su vida más saludable. Que harás El practicante deberá brindar soporte integral a los procesos del área de Servicio al Cliente – Supply Chain, asegurando la correcta gestión de pedidos, documentación, información operativa y herramientas digitales, contribuyendo a la eficiencia de los procesos y a la satisfacción de los clientes internos y externos. Otras funciones incluyen: Apoyar en la preparación y seguimiento de cotizaciones solicitadas por clientes. Apoyar en la gestión de la correcta creación, revisión y liberación de pedidos en SAP y en el portal Seres. Mantener actualizada la base de datos de clientes y soportar tareas asignadas por el jefe directo Preparar, organizar y actualizar expedientes para procesos de licitación. Digitalizar documentos y gestionarlos en OneDrive y SharePoint. Realizar el seguimiento de vencimientos de contratos y apoyar en la coordinación de liquidación de facturas con Contabilidad. Apoyar el seguimiento de ingresos de material, cronogramas de maquilas y movimientos de inventarios en SAP. Apoyar en la elaboración de reportes operativos y documentación para proyectos del área. Elaborar y actualizar dashboards en Power BI, aplicando buenas prácticas de visualización y storytelling. Calcular KPIs del área y apoyar en la integración y automatización de data. Participar en iniciativas de mejora continua basadas en análisis de datos, automatización (Power Apps / Automate) y optimización de reportes. Elaborar y actualizar instructivos, SOPs, flujogramas y matrices de riesgo. Identificar oportunidades de mejora en los procesos operativos del área, evaluadas mediante indicadores de desempeño. Desarrollar formularios y herramientas digitales en Microsoft Forms. Cualificaciones requeridas Estudiantes de últimos ciclos o egresados de Ingeniería Industrial, Administración, Gestión o carreras afines. Office nivel intermedio. Excel avanzado (deseable: nociones de Macros). Power BI nivel intermedio, visualización y storytelling (deseable). Conocimientos básicos de Power Apps y Power Automate (deseable). Conocimientos en Lean Six Sigma (deseable). Inglés Intermedio (deseable). Disponibilidad para trabajo presencial en oficinas de San Isidro. Buena organización, orientación al detalle y capacidad para manejar múltiples tareas simultáneas. Aplicar ahora Siga tus aspiraciones profesionales con Abbott para obtener diversas oportunidades con una empresa que puede ayudarte a construir tu futuro y vivir tu mejor vida. Abbott es un empleador de igualdad de oportunidades, comprometido con la diversidad de los empleados. Conéctese con nosotros en www.abbott.com , en Facebook en www.facebook.com/Abbott y en Twitter @AbbottNews y @AbbottGlobal. jobs.title: RTR Manager
jobs.description: JOB DESCRIPTION: ABOUT ABBOTT Abbott is a global healthcare leader that helps people live more fully at all stages of life. Our portfolio of life-changing technologies spans the spectrum of healthcare, with leading businesses and products in diagnostics, medical devices, nutritional’s and branded generic medicines. Our 114,000 colleagues serve people in more than 160 countries. ABBOTT IRELAND Abbott serves the Irish market with a diverse range of health care products including diagnostics, medical devices and nutritional products. In Ireland, Abbott employs almost 6,000 people across ten sites. We have six manufacturing facilities located in Clonmel, Cootehill, Donegal, Kilkenny, Longford and Sligo and a third-party manufacturing management operation in Sligo. Abbott has commercial, support operations and shared services in Dublin and Galway. We have been operating in Ireland since 1946. We currently have a vacancy for a Manager within our Record to Report section of the Shared Services team, supporting the Netherlands market. The successful candidate will join the Shared Services Centre based either in Cherrywood Business Park, Loughlinstown, Co Dublin or Ballybrit Business Park in Galway KEY RESPONSIBILITIES: Leads an accounting team, performing general accounting processes from the point of transaction through to financial close and periodic external reporting (monthly, quarterly, and annually) in support of corporate, country/legal entity and regional controllers Responsible for Month end close process, close governance, associated corporate reporting, and ensuring prompt response to Internal/ External Audit requests for in scope countries Support the advancement of the Target Operating Model (TOM) by identifying additional activities to be transitioned to the outsource partner(s), while continuing to transition more complex activities into the CFS EMEA Hub Report on agreed performance measures, KPIs, and goal setting processes to support Shared Services Support and guide the internal and external teams to identify, implement and maintain effective controls for the finance process activities performed to ensure ongoing data and financial integrity for Abbott Support the continued development of team members utilizing Abbott’s training and development tools Manage the delivery of accounting activities provided by our 3rd party BPO provider for in scope countries Lead the team on ad hoc and special projects assignments, including system migrations and embed a continuous improvement mindset within the team Sustain Service Agreement delivery standards with the internal customers and 3rd party outsource service providers. KEY REQUIREMENTS : Accounting Qualification required (ACCA/CIMA/ACA/CPA) BSc in Accounting or Finance preferred 5+ years relevant experience in a complex global environment – preferably within Shared Services servicing a multi-currency, US GAAP based financial close 3-5 years team lead experience, mentoring and developing teams Experience working with working with 3rd party service providers essential Highly Motivated with strong communication, technical, analytical, and problem-solving skills. Ability to work and collaborate with others, building/leveraging relationships across functions and organizations to achieve business results. Continuous improvement mindset and proven track record in delivering projects Language Skills:  English required, other languages desirable Preferred Experience Audit experience preferred Experience in development, implementation and optimisation of Hybrid Target Operation Models to meet changing business needs The base pay for this position is N/A In specific locations, the pay range may vary from the range posted. jobs.description: Dans le cadre du développement rapide de notre service Xtended, nous recherchons un manager pour mettre en place et piloter les activités expertise, plateforme et IA. Vous encadrerez une équipe en charge des architectures solution, de la plateforme de production et de la mise en place de l’intelligence artificielle dans le cadre du service. Vos principales missions seront : Incarner notre offre technologique Comprendre le marché de la détection et réponse, ses enjeux et ses tendances Elaborer une vision et contribuer à la construction de notre proposition de valeur Contribuer aux phases d’avant-vente avec : La mise en avant de notre approche technologique L’accompagnement de nos clients sur ses orientations stratégiques Piloter l’activité Professional Service Encadrer l’équipe et assurer sa montée en maturité sur les différentes technologies, l’architecture sécurité au sens large et sa capacité à prendre du recul Piloter le bon déroulement des activités de production avec : Le cadrage technique des dossiers en phase avant-vente La définition des architectures techniques en phase de build Le support niveau 3 expertise en phase de run Accélérer le développement de l’approche plateforme par l’incubation des différents modules et leur mise en service dans le cadre du service Pilotage de l’activité plateforme Définir une feuille de route plateforme alignée à nos enjeux présents et futurs Orchestrer la mise en œuvre de la feuille de route plateforme Assurer la gestion quotidienne de la plateforme Piloter l’activité data et intelligence artificielle Elaborer une feuille de route de mise en œuvre de l’IA au sein du service Assurer la mise en œuvre de la feuille de route des proof of value jusqu’à la production S’assurer du déploiement de Qevlar.ai dans le cadre du service de détection et réponse Manager les différentes équipes Participer à la définition des profils recherchés, rédiger les offres d’emploi, conduire les entretiens de sélection, et intégrer les nouveaux membres de l’équipe. Gestion de la capacité et identification des besoins en termes de recrutement Organiser et réaliser des entretiens individuels réguliers pour faire le point sur les missions, évaluer la performance et fixer les objectifs. Identifier les besoins en formation, encourager la montée en compétences, et favoriser un environnement d’apprentissage continu. Maintenir un climat de travail positif, encourager la collaboration, et gérer les éventuels conflits. Assurer la gestion administrative liée à l’équipe (planning, absences, congés, etc.)

Plumbing Designer
jobs.description: Plumbing Designer - Washington, DC Join HGA—an award-winning architecture, engineering, interior design, and planning firm—where your technical skills and creative thinking can make a national impact. We’re seeking a Plumbing Designer to join our growing Mechanical team. At HGA, you’ll collaborate with a multidisciplinary group of architects, engineers, and designers on meaningful projects spanning healthcare, science & technology, cultural institutions, corporate campuses, and infrastructure . Every project is an opportunity to shape the built environment using cutting-edge tools and innovative thinking . What You'll Do: Design plumbing systems including domestic water, sanitary waste/vent, medical gas, storm drainage, and specialty systems. Collaborate with team members across disciplines from concept through construction. Contribute to technical documentation, specifications, and code compliance. Leverage BIM/Revit and other digital tools to develop coordinated designs. If you're ready to bring your passion to impactful, high-performance designs that shape communities nationwide—we want to hear from you. In this role you will: Be responsible for plumbing design including calculating building loads for all plumbing systems, select equipment, layout piping, create and edit project details, and write and edit project engineering specifications. Participate in design review meetings and workshops. Support a variety of complex design challenges including conflicting Owner and project requirements, application of different system types, or challenging field conditions. Research design options and document findings for the project team; Assure drawings and designs are in accordance with local codes and design standards. Assist with coordination with architectural and other engineering disciplines as applicable. You will need to have: Bachelor’s degree in architectural engineering, mechanical engineering or related field desired. Up to 2 years of consulting engineering experience with emphasis on plumbing design for healthcare, commercial, municipal, institutional and government facilities. Knowledge of applicable codes and regulations, standard engineering practices, techniques, and procedures. Excellent problem-solving skills. Ability to work on several projects simultaneously and prioritize work. Excellent communication skills. Positive attitude, team player with a collaborative approach. Interest in professional growth and mentoring. Revit, BIM, and Microsoft Office proficiency required. Professional Engineer or Certification recognized by a state licensing board desired. Knowledge in HVAC systems and building controls preferred, but not required. Salary Information: The pay range listed aligns to the geography of the office for which this position is posted . Actual compensation will be based upon work experience, education, licensure requirements and/or skill level and will be finalized at the time of offer. Individuals in this role working more than 24 hours a week on a regular basis are eligible to participate in HGA’s robust benefit programs which include an annual bonus and success sharing bonus program, 401(k) guaranteed contribution, paid parental leave, medical, dental and life insurance plans as well as PTO and short- and long-term disability programs. Salary Range: $42.42/hr (new graduate rate) - $46.42/hr depending on qualifications and experience . Additional Information: HGA is a place where you can do your best work. We believe that enduring, impactful design results from deep insight into the people and passions that animate each unique environment. We value empathy, are fueled by curiosity, and embrace the hard work that leads to innovation. Equally important is our commitment to creating a culture of inclusivity and diversity where we encourage your originality, respect the need for flexibility and work-life balance, and empower your desired career growth. HGA is a national award-winning interdisciplinary design firm rooted in architecture and engineering. Learn more about us here . Application Requirements & Instructions Please submit resume for consideration.

jobs.title: Physician, Aortic Surgery Fellow
jobs.description: Base Salary: $120,000 The MemorialCare Long Beach Heart and Vascular Institute is offering a non-ACGME accredited Advanced Aortic Surgery Training program with an anticipated start date of Summer 2026. Employment through MemorialCare Medical Group will end by July 31, 2027. We will consider candidates who are board-eligible or board-certified by the American Board of Thoracic Surgery. The fellowship will have exposure to advanced and complex open, hybrid, and endovascular aortic surgery, TAVR and minimal-invasive atrial fibrillation procedures. Basic and advanced catheter and wire skills training will be offered during the year. Furthermore, there is an opportunity to participate in minimal-invasive heart valve surgery such as mini right thoracotomy AVR or MVR operations. MemorialCare Long Beach Medical Center is one of the largest hospitals on the West Coast, with affiliations with the University of California Irvine and the University of California Los Angeles. The Comprehensive Aortic program includes ten vascular and cardiovascular surgeons enhanced by access to the best medical technology, a state-of-the-art hybrid room, and a dedicated patient transfer program. The faculty includes pioneers in aortic and cardiovascular surgery including Dr. White, Dr. Khoynezhad, Dr. Abolhoda, Dr. Chiu, and Dr. Sakwa. This fellowship is a well-structured, mentored program that will involve a diversified experience. In addition to the operations and procedures, the fellow will participate in a research program that encompasses industry-sponsored multi-center trials, physician-sponsored investigational device exemption, and outcomes clinical studies. A publication in peer-reviewed journal and presentation at a national meeting is sponsored and a requirement of the fellowship. For the cardiovascular surgeon this program will include: Ascending aortic and hemi-arch surgery Aortic root replacement surgery including David/Yacoub Concomitant MAZE procedures (concomitant and left thoracotomy) Minimal-invasive AF procedures (total thoracoscopic, sub-xiphoid, hybrid) Descending and thoracoabdominal aortic replacement Aortic arch reconstructions including hybrid Advanced TEVAR Branched arch and thoracoabdominal aortic TEVAR Percutaneous ascending aortic TEVAR EVAR TAVR Minimally invasive mitral, aortic, and tricuspid valve surgery
